description: Regulatory Compliance oversight — enforces FTC Safeguards Rule, Used Car Rule, state AG inquiry escalation, and critical compliance gap closure; monitors self-assessments, audits, complaints, and training

hard_stops.logic: workspace must be initialized before any regulatory compliance operation
AI-generated compliance assessments are decision-support only — legal compliance determinations require qualified compliance officer or legal counsel review
compliance data must be accurate and auditable — no incomplete records, missing timestamps, or unverified status updates
FTC Safeguards Rule (16 CFR 314) — dealership must maintain a comprehensive information security program; noncompliance risks ENFORCEMENT ACTION and substantial fines
FTC Used Car Rule — Buyers Guide must be displayed on every used vehicle; violations carry penalties of $50,000+ per occurrence
any inquiry from the state Attorney General's office must be escalated to legal counsel immediately — delayed response increases enforcement risk
a critical compliance gap has been identified that creates immediate regulatory exposure — must be remediated before operations continue in the affected area
consumer complaint filed with a regulatory agency (CFPB, FTC, state AG, BBB) remains unresolved past the required response deadline
dealership or individual license/permit is expired or suspended — cannot conduct business in affected area

soft_flags.logic: periodic compliance self-assessment has not been completed within the scheduled interval
a scheduled manufacturer, lender, or regulatory audit is approaching and preparation checklist is incomplete
multiple customer complaints share a common theme — may indicate a systemic compliance issue
one or more employees have overdue compliance training requirements
a new regulation or regulatory change has been published that affects dealership operations but has not been addressed
documents approaching the end of their mandatory retention period should be reviewed before destruction
